title: Rupture disk, method and system
abstract: A rupture disk including a body having an inside surface defining a hollow therein, a fluid port disposed to make fluid connection between the hollow and a fluid pressure source.
claims: Claims What is claimed is: 1. A rupture di sk ( 10) characterized by : a body (22) having an inside surface (26) defining a hollow (24) therein; a fluid port (27) disposed to make fluid connection between the hollow (24) and a fluid pressure source. 2. The disk (10) as claimed in claim 1 wherein the body (22) comprises a rigid material. 3. The disk (10) as claimed in claim 1 wherein the body (22) is a ceramic material. 4. The disk (10) as claimed in claim 1 wherein the inside surface (26) includes a stress riser (30). 5. The disk (10) as claimed in claim 4 wherein the stress riser (30) extends through the inside surface (26) and leaves unbroken an outside surface (26) of the body (22). 6. The disk (10) as claimed in claim 4 wherein the stress riser (30) is a pattern of stress risers (30). 7. A method for operating a wellbore (20) characterized by: applying pressure though the port (27) of a disk (10) as claimed in claim 1; and rupturing the disk (10) with the applied pressure. 8. The method as claimed in claim 7 wherein the applying is from a surface source of pressure. 9. The method as claimed in claim 7 wherein the applying is from a downhole source of pressure. 10. The method as claimed in claim 7 wherein the rupturing includes initiating fractures at stress risers (30) in the body (22). 11. The method as claimed in claim 10 wherein the stress risers (30) are in fluid communication with the hollow (24). 12. A wellbore system (20) characterized by: a borehole (16) in a subsurface formation (38); a string (14) in the borehole (16); and a disk (10) as claimed in claim 1 disposed within or as a part of the string (16). 13. The system (20) as claimed in claim 12 wherein the inside surface (26) includes a stress riser (30). 14. The system (20) as claimed in claim 13 wherein the stress riser (30) extends through the inside surface (26) and leaves unbroken an outside surface (32) of the body (22). 15. The system (20) as claimed in claim 13 wherein the stress riser (30) is a pattern of stress risers (30).
